16.4% ([[Nielsen ratings]]){{cite web |title=Academy Awards ratings |publisher=[[Television Bureau of Advertising]] |url=https://www.tvb.org/Portals/0/media/file/tracts/Academy_Awards.pdf |access-date=July 16, 2019 |archive-date=August 8, 2016 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160808235818/https://www.tvb.org/Portals/0/media/file/tracts/Academy_Awards.pdf |url-status=live }} | last = 90th | next = 92nd }} The '''91st Academy Awards''' ceremony, presented by the [[Academy of Motion Picture Arts and Sciences]] (AMPAS), honored the best [[2018 in film|films of 2018]] and took place on February 24, 2019, at the [[Dolby Theatre]] in [[Hollywood, Los Angeles]]. During the ceremony, AMPAS presented [[Academy Awards]] (commonly referred to as Oscars) in 24 categories. The ceremony was televised in the United States by [[American Broadcasting Company|ABC]] and was produced by [[Donna Gigliotti]] and [[Glenn Weiss]], with Weiss also serving as director.{{cite news Butler]] and [[David Shirk]] ** ''[[Solo: A Star Wars Story]]'' – [[Rob Bredow]], [[Patrick Tubach]], [[Neal Scanlan]] and [[Dominic Tuohy]] |} === Governors Awards === The Academy held its 10th annual [[Governors Awards]] ceremony on November 18, 2018, where the following awards were presented:{{cite news|first=Pete|last=Hammond|title=Governors Awards Honorees: Kathleen Kennedy, Frank Marshall, Marvin Levy, Lalo Schifrin & Cicely Tyson|url=https://deadline.com/2018/09/governors-awards-2018-kathleen-kennedy-frank-marshall-marvin-levy-lalo-schifrin-cicely-tyson-movie-academy-1202457893/|work=Deadline Hollywood|date=September 5, 2018|access-date=September 5, 2018|url-status=live|archive-url= https://web.archive.org/web/20170220104835/https://deadline.com/2018/09/governors-awards-2018-kathleen-kennedy-frank-marshall-marvin-levy-lalo-schifrin-cicely-tyson-movie-academy-1202457893/|archive-date=February 20, 2017}} ====Honorary Awards==== * To Marvin Levy, for an exemplary career in publicity that has brought films to the minds, hearts and souls of audiences all over the world.{{cite web |title=Academy Awards Acceptance Speech Database |url=http://awardsdatabase.oscars.org/Search/GetResults?query=%7B%22AwardShowFrom%22:91,%22Sort%22:%223-Award%20Category-Chron%22,%22Search%22:%22Basic%22%7D |publisher=Academy of Motion Picture Arts and Sciences |access-date=21 May 2019 |archive-date=December 18, 2020 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20201218083404/http://awardsdatabase.oscars.org/Search/GetResults?query=%7B%22AwardShowFrom%22%3A91%2C%22Sort%22%3A%223-Award+Category-Chron%22%2C%22Search%22%3A%22Basic%22%7D |url-status=live }} * To [[Lalo Schifrin]], in recognition of his unique musical style, compositional integrity and influential contributions to the art of film scoring. * To [[Cicely Tyson]], whose unforgettable performances and personal integrity have inspired generations of filmmakers, actors and audiences. ====Irving G. Thalberg Memorial Award==== The award honors "creative producers whose bodies of work reflect a consistently high quality of motion picture production".{{cite news|title=Irvin G. Thalberg Memorial Award |url=http://www.oscars.org/governors/thalberg |access-date=December 18, 2015 |publisher=Academy of Motion Picture Arts and Sciences |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160101224449/http://oscars.org/governors/thalberg |archive-date=January 1, 2016 }} * [[Kathleen Kennedy (producer)|Kathleen Kennedy]] and [[Frank Marshall (producer)|Frank Marshall]].{{cite magazine|first=Piya|last=Sinha-Roy|title=Cicely Tyson, Kathleen Kennedy break new ground with honorary Oscars|url=https://ew.com/awards/2018/11/19/governors-awards-2018-cicely-tyson-kathleen-kennedy/|magazine=Entertainment Weekly|date=September 5, 2018|access-date=September 5, 2018|url-status=live|archive-url= https://web.archive.org/web/20170220104835/https://ew.com/awards/2018/11/19/governors-awards-2018-cicely-tyson-kathleen-kennedy/|archive-date=February 20, 2017}} ===Films with multiple nominations and awards=== {{col-float}} {| class="wikitable plainrowheaders" style="text-align: center;" |+ Films with multiple nominations |- ! Star Is Born]]'' |- | [[Los Angeles Philharmonic]] || Performers || "Leaving Home" during the annual "In Memoriam" tribute |- |} == Ceremony information == In October 2018, the Academy hired film producer [[Donna Gigliotti]] and television producer [[Glenn Weiss]] to oversee production of the 2019 ceremony.{{cite news |last1=Kilday |first1=Gregg |title=Donna Gigliotti, Glenn Weiss to Produce 91st Academy Awards |url=https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/news/oscars-donna-gigliotti-glenn-weiss-produce-91st-academy-awards-1154276 |access-date=October 22, 2018 |work=The Hollywood Reporter "Donna and Glenn will infuse new energy and vision into this 91st awards presentation, and we are excited about a broad-based creative relationship with these two artists", Academy President [[John Bailey (cinematographer)|John Bailey]] said in a press release announcing the decision.{{cite news |url=https://deadline.com/2018/10/donna-gigliotti-to-produce-91st-oscars-glenn-weiss-is-co-producer-1202487422/ |title=Donna Gigliotti to Produce 91st Oscars; Glenn Weiss Is Co-Producer |date=October 22, 2018 |access-date=October 22, 2018 |work=Deadline Hollywood |first1=Erik |last1=Pedersen |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160924190214/http://deadline.com/2018/10/donna-gigliotti-to-produce-91st-oscars-glenn-weiss-is-co-producer-1202487422/ |archive-date=September 24, 2016}} Furthermore, AMPAS CEO [[Dawn Hudson]] added, "We're thrilled to work with someone as passionate about the Academy as Donna Gigliotti — an Oscar winner and multiple nominee. She and the now-famous Glenn Weiss are committed to making the most of the innovations we've embraced for our 91st Oscars." In response, co-producer Gigliotti expressed gratitude saying, "I'm grateful to the Academy and ABC for entrusting me with this very special opportunity. I'm sure it will prove to be a humbling, exhilarating and completely unique experience. Thankfully, I will have Glenn Weiss by my side every step of the way!" Weiss also released a statement which read, "I'm so looking forward to being back to help celebrate and honor the film industry and all the talented people behind this year's movies. And I am 'co-excited' to be co-producing this year's show with Donna Gigliotti!" Two months later, Gigliotti and Weiss initially selected actor and comedian [[Kevin Hart]] to host the ceremony.{{cite news |last1=Nellie |first1=Andreeva |last2=Patrick |first2=Hipes |title=Kevin Film" category=== Due to the declining viewership of the recent Academy Awards ceremonies, AMPAS sought new ideas to revamp the show while renewing interest with the nominated films. In August 2018, the organization announced plans to add a new category honoring achievement in "[[Academy Award for Outstanding Achievement in Popular Film|Popular Film]]".{{cite news |last1=Feinberg |first1=Scott |title=Oscars Won't Televise All Awards Live, Adds "Popular" Film Category |url=https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/race/academy-plans-three-hour-oscars-telecast-adds-popular-film-category-1133138 |access-date=September 22, 2019 |work=The Hollywood Reporter |date=August 8, 2018 |archive-date=November 3, 2016 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20161103125108/https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/race/academy-plans-three-hour-oscars-telecast-adds-popular-film-category-1133138 |url-status=live }} The proposal was met with criticism because the award's implied focus on [[Blockbuster (entertainment)|blockbuster]] suggested that [[art film|artistic films]] and other non-mainstream pictures were not "popular with audiences".{{cite news |last1=Benson-Allott |first1=Caitlin |title=The Oscars have always been crass. Its new award for popular film just embraces that. |url=https://www.washingtonpost.com/news/posteverything/wp/2018/08/09/the-oscars-have-always-been-crass-its-new-award-for-popular-film-just-embraces-that/ |access-date=July 16, 2019 |newspaper=The Washington Post |date=August 9, 2018 |archive-date=January 14, 2020 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200114233617/https://www.washingtonpost.com/news/posteverything/wp/2018/08/09/the-oscars-have-always-been-crass-its-new-award-for-popular-film-just-embraces-that/ |url-status=live }}{{cite news |last1=Tapley |first1=Kristopher |title=Academy Members React to 'Popular' Oscar News: 'The Film Business Passed Away Today' |url=https://variety.com/2018/film/news/oscars-popular-film-category-academy-members-react-1202900278/ |access-date=November 5, 2019 |work=Variety |date=August 8, 2018 |archive-date=August 26, 2018 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180826171151/https://variety.com/2018/film/news/oscars-popular-film-category-academy-members-react-1202900278/ |url-status=live }}{{cite magazine|last=Zacharek|first=Stephanie|author-link=Stephanie Zacharek|url=https://time.com/5361417/new-oscar-category-popular-film/|title=The Oscars' New 'Popular Film' Category Confirms That Hollywood Thinks We're Stupid|magazine=[[Time (magazine)|Time]]|date=August 8, 2018|access-date=October 22, 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180809104704/http://time.com/5361417/new-oscar-category-popular-film/|archive-date=August 9, 2018|url-status=live}} Furthermore, many viewed the creation of this new category to be a ploy to boost ratings and that it could hamper critically successful mainstream films from being nominated for Best Picture despite the insistence of AMPAS that such movies could be eligible for both categories.{{cite news |last1=Chang |first1=Justin |author-link1=Justin Chang |title=How misguided is the academy's new 'popular film' Oscars category? 2018 |archive-date=October 30, 2019 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191030222106/https://www.latimes.com/entertainment/movies/la-et-mn-popular-film-oscar-critic-20180810-story.html Chuck Barney of ''[[The Mercury News]]'' commented, "After months of bad buzz and embarrassing missteps, the 91st Academy Awards appeared to be on their way to an epic fail." He also remarked, "The show, as usual, had some rocky moments, but overall it was a lively, well-paced and entertaining affair."{{cite news |last1=Barney |first1=Chuck |title=Academy Awards telecast an epic fail? Surprisingly not |url=https://www.mercurynews.com/2019/02/24/academy-awards-telecast-an-epic-fail-surprisingly-not/ |access-date=July 16, 2019 |work=[[The Mercury News]] |date=February 24, 2019 |archive-date=March 1, 2019 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190301205116/https://www.mercurynews.com/2019/02/24/academy-awards-telecast-an-epic-fail-surprisingly-not/ |url-status=live }} Television critic Matthew Gilbert of ''[[The Boston Globe]]'' wrote, "The hostless Oscar ceremony felt a little more streamlined and energetic than usual, as we were spared yet another predictable opening goof on the nominated movies, a monologue pretending to make fun of the stars, and short quips across the night that never quite land."{{cite news |last1=Gilbert |first1=Matthew |title=Even minus a host, Oscars are a little much |url=https://www.bostonglobe.com/arts/television/2019/02/24/host-problem-broadcast-winner/fcgM07AvD4bmZHTrUZKi9I/story.html |access-date=March 7, 2019 |work=The Boston Globe |date=February 24, 2019 |archive-date=March 2, 2019 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190302125924/https://www.bostonglobe.com/arts/television/2019/02/24/host-problem-broadcast-winner/fcgM07AvD4bmZHTrUZKi9I/story.html |url-status=live }} ''[[The Hollywood Reporter]]'' television critic Daniel Fienberg quipped, "Sunday's Oscars telecast definitely confirmed that under the right circumstances, a host isn't a necessity." In addition, he said, "The show was not overwhelmed by montages or stunts or tributes, and it also wasn't weighed down by elaborately prepared presenter schtick."{{cite news |last1=Fienberg |first1=Daniel |title=91st Academy Awards: TV Review |url=https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/review/91st-academy-awards-tv-review-emotional-speeches-steal-show-1190282 |access-date=May 21, 2019 |work=The Hollywood Reporter |date=February 24, 2019 |archive-date=May 16, 2019 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190516144853/https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/review/91st-academy-awards-tv-review-emotional-speeches-steal-show-1190282 |url-status=live }} Others were more critical of the show. Kelly Lawler of ''[[USA Today]]'' wrote, "The 2019 Oscar telecast lacked energy and comedy, and was treacherously dull. And maybe that could have been avoided if the Academy had done what it has (almost) always done, and procured a host." She also observed that the awards "were more like a press conference with movie clips in the middle".{{cite news |last1=Lawler |first1=Kelly |title=The Oscars became a press conference with movie clips. Note to Academy: Get a host next time |url=https://www.usatoday.com/story/life/tv/2019/02/25/oscars-2019-painfully-boring-awards-show-needed-host/2977034002/ |access-date=September 22, 2019 |work=USA Today |date=February 25, 2019 |archive-date=November 11, 2019 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191111073725/https://www.usatoday.com/story/life/tv/2019/02/25/oscars-2019-painfully-boring-awards-show-needed-host/2977034002/ |url-status=live }} Television critic [[Hank Stuever]] of ''[[The Washington Post]]'' commented, "They kept all the speeches but lost any trace of the unpredictable magic. They opened with [[Queen (band)|Queen]] (the actual band, fronted by their usual [[Freddie Mercury]] replacement, [[Adam Lambert]]) and a promise that '[[We Will Rock You]],' but we all know better. The Oscar telecast has never rocked anyone." He ended his review stating, "It's painful, year after year, to watch show business struggle to find a better way to put on a satisfying show."{{cite news |last1=Stuever |first1=Hank |title=Oscar keeps it shorter without a host, but something still needs to done about those speeches |url=https://www.washingtonpost.com/entertainment/tv/oscar-keeps-it-shorter-without-a-host-but-something-still-needs-to-done-about-those-speeches/2019/02/25/834aa124-388a-11e9-a06c-3ec8ed509d15_story.html |access-date=November 5, 2019 |newspaper=The Washington Post |date=February 24, 2019 |archive-date=December 31, 2019 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191231005213/https://www.washingtonpost.com/entertainment/tv/oscar-keeps-it-shorter-without-a-host-but-something-still-needs-to-done-about-those-speeches/2019/02/25/834aa124-388a-11e9-a06c-3ec8ed509d15_story.html |url-status=live }} The American telecast on ABC drew in an average of 29.56 million people over its length, which was a 12% increase from the previous year's ceremony. The show also earned higher [[Nielsen ratings]] compared to the previous ceremony with 16.4% of households watching the ceremony. In addition, it garnered a higher 18–49 demo rating with a 7.7 rating among viewers in that demographic.{{cite news|last=Patten|first=Dominic|title=Oscar Ratings Up From 2018 To 29.6M Viewers With
